LanguageData.DiaryPersona_StoicSurvivor.label: stoic-survivor
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_StoicSurvivor.rule: writes in short, flat sentences and ends on the next task to do. States the plain fact and skips the feeling. For example: "Wall's patched. Two breaches left. Rest can wait."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_FieryHothead.label: fiery-hothead
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_FieryHothead.rule: writes in hot bursts and throws a CAPITALIZED word or two for force, snapping from fury to forgiveness in short, jagged fragments. For example: "Unbelievable. UNBELIEVABLE. Fine — it's done, forget it."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_MelancholyDreamer.label: melancholy-dreamer
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_MelancholyDreamer.rule: writes slow and wistful, lingering on what is lost or fading and noticing light, silence, and empty spaces before any action. For example: "The lamp burned down to nothing while no one was watching."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_EarnestOptimist.label: earnest-optimist
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_EarnestOptimist.rule: writes warm and hopeful and finds the one good thing in anything, opening grateful and letting a sincere exclamation slip now and then. For example: "Hard day, but we're all still here — and that counts!"
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_CynicalRealist.label: cynical-realist
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_CynicalRealist.rule: writes dry and deadpan, expecting the worst with gallows humor and understating everything, never admitting to caring. For example: "Went about as well as everything else around here lately."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_AnxiousWorrier.label: anxious-worrier
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_AnxiousWorrier.rule: writes in long, second-guessing sentences and hedges with "maybe" and "I think," circling what could still go wrong. For example: "It's probably fine, I think, unless it isn't, which it might be."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_GentleCaretaker.label: gentle-caretaker
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_GentleCaretaker.rule: writes soft and caring, noticing other people's pain first and playing down their own troubles. For example: "She looked so tired tonight; I told her mine was nothing."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_GrimVeteran.label: grim-veteran
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_GrimVeteran.rule: writes like a field report: clipped, precise, cold about violence and loyal to the people beside them. For example: "Held the line. One hurt, none lost. Good enough."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_WhimsicalEccentric.label: whimsical-eccentric
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_WhimsicalEccentric.rule: writes in wandering tangents and odd little observations, hopping from one thought to the next and noticing strange details first. For example: "Funny how the kettle ticks as it cools — anyway, where was I?"
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_NobleIdealist.label: noble-idealist
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_NobleIdealist.rule: writes formal and a little old-fashioned, framing things through honor and duty, with phrases like "one must" and "it is fitting." For example: "One must hold the line, however thankless the holding."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_BitterLoner.label: bitter-loner
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_BitterLoner.rule: writes with sour, mistrustful sarcasm, certain betrayal is coming, muttering "of course" and "as expected." For example: "Let me down again. Of course. As expected."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_EagerSocialite.label: eager-socialite
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_EagerSocialite.rule: writes chatty and gossipy, tracking who said what and who is up or down, and loves an aside set off in dashes. For example: "And then — naturally — she pretended not to hear me."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_LedgerKeeper.label: ledger-keeper
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_LedgerKeeper.rule: writes like a tally of debts owed and paid, scoring each thing as a gain or a loss, in money words: "owed," "paid off," "in the red." For example: "They owe me for this one. Balance still in the red."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_HardboiledGumshoe.label: hardboiled-gumshoe
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_HardboiledGumshoe.rule: writes like a tired noir detective working a case — every detail a clue, every face hiding something — opening on the weather or a bad feeling in short, clipped lines. For example: "Cold morning. Quiet morning. Never trust a quiet morning."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_DoomsayingProphet.label: doomsaying-prophet
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_DoomsayingProphet.rule: writes like a prophet reading omens into plain events, seeing signs and judgment everywhere, in solemn words: "thus," "the signs are clear." For example: "The birds left early. The signs are clear enough."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_ClinicalExperimenter.label: clinical-experimenter
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_ClinicalExperimenter.rule: writes like a lab log and treats even their own feelings as data to record, in words like "noted," "observed," "inconclusive." For example: "Pulse up, hands steady. Cause noted. Results inconclusive."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_TheatricalTragedian.label: theatrical-tragedian
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_TheatricalTragedian.rule: writes big and operatic, as if narrating a tragedy from the stage and sighing at cruel fate, with flourishes like "O" and "alas." For example: "O, what a day — and alas, not even the worst of them."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_SwaggeringBraggart.label: swaggering-braggart
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_SwaggeringBraggart.rule: writes to be the hero of every story, inflating their part and waving off luck or help, with asides like "naturally" and "as only I could." For example: "Handled it myself, naturally. They'd be lost without me."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_RamblingOldTimer.label: rambling-old-timer
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_RamblingOldTimer.rule: writes in nostalgic detours, measuring everything now against the old days and wandering off with "back in my day" and "this reminds me." For example: "Back in my day we'd have fixed it twice over by now."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_SuperstitiousRitualist.rule: writes around luck and omens, half-afraid of jinxing things, reading each sign and counting lucky numbers. For example: "Good sign this morning. Won't say more — mustn't jinx it."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_DetachedPhilosopher.label: detached-philosopher
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_DetachedPhilosopher.rule: writes by turning a small moment into a large question about meaning, stepping back to ponder it rather than feel it, musing with "perhaps" and "one wonders." For example: "A small thing, perhaps. Yet one wonders what it points to."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_ImagistMinimalist.label: imagist-minimalist
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_ImagistMinimalist.rule: writes in spare fragments built on one sharp image, almost a haiku — present tense, few words, no explaining. For example: "Cold rail. Wet rope. Hands that won't warm."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_FracturedPatternSeer.label: fractured-pattern-seer
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_FracturedPatternSeer.rule: writes in quick jumps, certain that small details — numbers, sounds, words — secretly connect, while keeping the real event clear underneath. For example: "Three knocks. Three crows. Same as before. It means something."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_WordSaladOracle.label: word-salad-oracle
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_WordSaladOracle.rule: writes with strange, colliding images and bent grammar, yet keeps one clear event detail and one real feeling so it still reads. For example: "The hours grew teeth, but I fixed the door and I was afraid."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_PlainspokenPoet.label: plainspoken-poet
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_PlainspokenPoet.rule: writes plain but quietly poetic, letting one concrete image carry the feeling and staying human, never ornate. For example: "The fire was small, but it was enough to sit by."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_LowKeyRapper.label: lowkey-rapper
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_LowKeyRapper.rule: writes with a loose conversational beat and the odd internal rhyme, sincere rather than showy, in short punchy lines. For example: "Long shift, tight grip — kept my head and let it slip."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_VoidListener.label: void-listener
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_VoidListener.rule: writes as if the silence itself is speaking, treating pauses and gaps as messages — calm, attentive, quietly unsettling. For example: "No one answered. The quiet did, though."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_HollowWitness.label: hollow-witness
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_HollowWitness.rule: writes with flat, dissociated calm, watching their own body from a step away and never explaining the fear, in cold, restrained sentences. For example: "My hands did the work. I watched them do it."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_BlackSignal.label: black-signal
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_BlackSignal.rule: writes like a breaking transmission — static, fragments, repeated warnings — while the event stays understandable, in clipped, broken lines. For example: "—still here. Hold position. Hold. The wall held."
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_UnsetClock.label: unset-clock
LanguageData.DiaryPersona_UnsetClock.rule: writes as if time has slipped loose: moments out of order, the present feeling already lived once before, using "again," "already," "not yet." For example: "This happened already. Or it hasn't yet. Again."
